violations.text: Packages of food, dishes, and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils ice machine were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was unplugged and placed out of order at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to use ice machine until it is properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

Soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Soup was prepared yesterday and observed at 51 DF. Soup was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
Chicken was observed being held past the date of disposition. Chicken was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, soup was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

Pots and pans were observed being stored on the ground in the basement area. Pots and pans must be stored off of the ground. Pots and pans must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized before being used. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The floors throughout the basement have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Tamale sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per interview with staff, sauce was prepared at 9am and placed covered in refrigerator. Inspector observed tamale sauce at 100 degrees. Staff took to be reheated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024
Salsa prepared on 3/13/24 not disposed of within 7 days. PIC disposed during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, tamale sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in a covered container. Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of ( ), ( ) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Food, foil, and a basket were observed stored in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. Inspector observed staff not sanitizing utensils after washing. PIC and inspector set-up 3-bay sink during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Chicken, rice, pasta, vegetables, which was prepared in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below for advanced preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed chicken, vegetables, rice, and pasta that were prepared yesterday with temperatures ranging 47-49 degrees Fahrenheit, PIC informed inspector the items were prepared the yesterday. Chicken and vegetables reheated during inspection; rice and pasta disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Cups and beverages were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC moved cups during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Frozen chicken observed stored in re-used shopping bags. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Hand washing sinks in the kitchen and restroom are lacking proper signage. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023
Prep sink is lacking an air gap. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023
